final_answer: COVID-19 spreads mainly through inhaling air contaminated by droplets and aerosols from infected individuals. Infected individuals exhale virus-containing particles when they breathe, talk, cough, sneeze, or sing. Transmission is most likely at close range but can occur over longer distances, especially indoors. COVID-19 can also spread when infectious particles come into contact with the eyes, nose, or mouth. Additionally, touching surfaces contaminated with the virus and then touching the face can lead to transmission. People can remain contagious for up to 20 days and may spread the virus without showing symptoms.
